ogTitle: Turvey Park vs Tweed Heads: Climate Safety

heading: Turvey Park vs Tweed Heads — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Turvey Park, New South Wales and Tweed Heads, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Turvey Park has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Tweed Heads (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Turvey Park or Tweed Heads safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Turvey Park has a flood risk score of 20/100, while Tweed Heads scores 20/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Turvey Park scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Tweed Heads scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Turvey Park or Tweed Heads?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Turvey Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
